original: Emperor Lo-Yang of China sees supernova MSH 15-52
Margaret II Countess of Hainaut and her son William, Duke of Bavaria, sign a peace treaty ending the Cod and Hook wars between them
Connecticut Route 108, one of the oldest highways in the U.S. is completed to Trumbull
Great storm of 1703 hits Southern England - thousands killed, Royal Navy losses 13 ships and around 1,500 seamen
The first Eddystone Lighthouse is destroyed in the Great Storm of 1703
Tumult of Thorn - religious unrest is followed by the execution of nine Protestant citizens and the mayor of Thorn (Toruń) by Polish authorities
The Royal Opera House opens at Covent Garden, London, England
Elisabeth Petrovna becomes Tsarina of Russia
Bavarian Ludwig railway opens - first German steam railway
New York Philharmonic's 1st concert
Battle of Prairie Grove, Arkansas ends in a stalemate; Confederate forces withdraw, leaving Union troops in control of northwest Arkansas
Skirmish at Ebenezer Creek/Cypress Swamp, Georgia
HMS Challenger sets sail on 3½ year world oceanographic cruise - first  expedition to explore the deep sea successfully on a global scale
Natives Sons of the West organized
NY Mutuals & Philadelphia A's expelled from NL for not completing sked
49th Congress (1885-87) convenes
52nd US Congress (1st to appropriate $1 billion) holds 1st session
Battle at Amba Alagi: Abyssinians beat Italian armies
Max Planck, in his house at Grunewald, on the outskirts of Berlin, discovers the law of black body radiation.
Britain and Germany issue an ultimatum to Venezuela demanding that President Cipriano Castro pay claims for damages caused during his takeover of the government in 1899
Eugene Corri becomes 1st referee in a boxing ring
The First Egyptian Nationalist Congress meets under the leadership of Mustafa Kamil Pasha
Leslie J Stuart's musical "Betsy" premieres in NYC
National Hockey Association forms with New Westminster, Vancouver & Victoria
Bust of Queen Nefertiti is found in El-Amarna, Egypt
US becomes 13th country to declare war on Austria during World War I
NSW make 802 against South Australia, then Mailey takes 8-81
USPD-KPD parties merge into Vereinigte Communist Party of Germany
KWG-AM in Stockton CA begins radio transmissions
German election (Social Democrats win, Nazis & Communists lose)
Biltmore Theater opens at 261 W 47th St NYC
Gas refrigerator patented
1st gyro-stabilized vessel to cross Atlantic arrives in NY
Dutch Minister Romme proclaims married women are forbidden to work
Russian chess player Alexander Alekhine recaptures his final world title from Dutch grand master Max Euwe by a large margin (+10−4=11)
Philip Barry's play "Here Come the Clowns" premieres in NYC
W9XZY broadcasts facsimile of St Louis Post-Dispatch by radio
William Walton's Violin Concerto premieres in Cleveland; Ohio, with Jascha Heifetz as soloist and the Cleveland Orchestra conducted by Artur Rodziński
North Africa: British counter offensive under general O'Connor
The first prototype Fairey Barracuda flew
1st Japanese midget submarine (No. 20) attacked by a US ship (USS Ward)
Australian bombers land on Timor and Ambon islands of Indonesia
Imperial Japanese Navy attacks US Naval Air Station Kaneohe Bay, Hawaii; 18 sailors killed, 33 of 36 patrol planes destroyed or damaged, 1st Japanese aircraft shot down in action
USS New Jersey launched, built by Philadelphia Naval Shipyard - US's most decorated warship (decommissioned 1991)
Convention on International Civil Aviation drawn up in Chicago
General Radescu forms Romanian government
Fire at Winecoff Hotel in Atlanta, kills 119
15th Heisman Trophy Award: Leon Hart (end), Notre Dame
KKTV TV channel 11 in Colorado Spgs-Pueblo, CO (CBS) 1st broadcast
WCCB TV channel 18 in Charlotte, North Carolina (IND/ABC) begins broadcasting
Japanese government of Joshida resigns
KCTS TV channel 9 in Seattle, WA (PBS) begins broadcasting
American diver Pat McCormick wraps up the women's double when she wins the 10m platform gold medal at the Melbourne Olympics with 84.85 points; also won the 3m springboard gold
Australian swimmer Murray Rose claims his 3rd gold medal at the Melbourne Olympics when he wins the 1,500m in 17:58.9; also wins 400m & 4 x 200m relay
Helen O'Connell joins Today Show panel (NBC)
Tony Kubek of the Yanks selected as AL Rookie of the Year
Romulo Betancourt elected President of Venezuela
Ivory Coast claims independence from France
Great Britain performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
First use of the instant replay machine invented by CBS in a US Army vs Navy football game
Ian Meckiff no-balled for throwing against the South Africans
A fire at an army barracks in Erzurum, Turkey kills 68 people.
The Beatles' clothing store "Apple Boutique" opens at 94 Baker Street, London
Orbiting Astronomical Observatory 2 launched into Earth orbit
Richard Dodd returns a library book his great-grandfather took out in 1823 from the University of Cincinnati
Taizan Maezumi Roshi, head of LA Zen Center, receives dharma
West Germany & Poland normalize relations
An off-duty member of the Ulster Defence Regiment is shot dead by members of the Irish Republican Army in County Tyrone, Northern Ireland
Wings release their 1st album "Wild Life"
Orioles sell pitcher Eddie Watt to the Phillies
10th Islander shut-out opponent-Glenn Resch 3-0 vs Sabres
Archbishop Makarios returns to Cyprus
Indonesian army occupies East Timor
Islander Billy Smith's 10th shut-out opponent-Black Hawks 4-0
Spain becomes a member of the NATO
Dos Erres massacre in Guatemala during country's civil war, 171 people killed led by Santos López Alonzo
In Texas, Charles Brooks, Jr. becomes the first person to be executed by lethal injection in the US.
Suriname army under Desi Bouterse fires on radio station building
2 jets collided at Madrid Airport killing 93
France performs nuclear test at Mururoa Atoll
Atlantis (61-B) returns to Kennedy Space Center via Kelly AFB
NBC premiere of miniseries "Anastasia: The Mystery of Anna" (Part 1)
43 die in Pacific Southwest Airline crash in California (man shot pilots)
Palestinian uprising against Israel in West Bank
A 6.9 magnitude earthquake in Spitak, Armenia, kills 25,000 to 50,000 people and leaves up to 500,000 homeless
American visual artist Chuck Close (48) suffers a spinal artery collapse leaving him paralyzed from the neck down
Gorbachevannounces 10% unilateral Soviet troop reductions at UN
Mikhail Gorbachevcheered by Wall St crowds upon arrival in NYC
C Coleman & D Zippel's musical "City of Angels" premieres in NYC
A J Kitt, US, wins World Skiing Cup
Galileo spacecraft passes North Pole of Moon (Peary Crater)
Henri Konan Bedie names himself President of Ivory coast
The Long Island Rail Road massacre: Passenger Colin Ferguson murders six people and injures 19 others on the LIRR in Nassau County, New York.
NBA settles strike of referees, refs to return on Dec 12
US space probe Galileo begins orbiting Jupiter

Australian rugby union team ends 12-game European tour unbeaten after 39-19 victory over the Barbarians at Twickenham; Wallabies fullback Matt Burke scores 2 tries and lands 4 conversions, 2 penalties
Space Shuttle STS 80 (Columbia 21), lands
The RIAA files a lawsuit against the Napster file-sharing client, on charges of copyright infringement.
The Conservative Party of Canada is officially recognized after the merger of the Canadian Alliance and Progressive Conservative Party of Canada.
Rigoberto Alpizar, a passenger on American Airlines Flight 924 who allegedly claimed to have a bomb, is shot and killed by a team of US federal air marshals at Miami International Airport.
A Tornado strikes Kensal Green in northwest London, seriously damaging around 150 properties
The Hebei Spirit oil spill began in South Korea after a crane barge being towed by tug collided with the very large crude carrier, Hebei Spirit.
"The NewsHour with Jim Lehrer" becomes "PBS NewsHour", upon Jim Lehrer's stepping down as anchor
Iceland officially leaves the recession with growth of 1.2% in the third quarter
Human Rights Watch Asia Division call for investigation into Indonesian security forces shooting of peaceful protesters
Mayor of Paris calls for diesel cars to be banned from the French Capital by 2020, in order to reduce pollution
The Archbishop of Canterbury claims that he is more shocked by the plight of Britain's hunger-stricken poor than suffering in African refugee camps
The Syrian military accuse Israel of carrying out two air strikes on Syria
Typhoon Hagupit hits central Philippines; millions flee homes for shelter
Beijing issues its 1st ever red alert for pollution
6.5 earthquake in Aceh province, Indonesia kills at least 97 people
Italian Prime Minister Matteo Renzi resigns 3 days after losing a referendum to reform the constitution
Pakistan International Airways plane crashes north of Islamabad, killing all 48 on board, including singer turned preacher Junaid Jamshed
Australia's parliament votes to legalize gay marriage
Crumbling French château La Mothe-Chandeniers in Les Trois-Moutiers is bought by 9,000 people
Former US gymnastics physician Larry Nasser is sentenced to 60 years on child pornography charges
Mosconi Cup nine-ball pool, Las Vegas, Nevada: Europe scores 8th straight title with an 11-4 rout of US; MVP: Joshua Filler (Germany)
Naples' pizza spinning given UNESCO intangible heritage status along with Germany's organ music, Kyrgystan's Kok boru, and India's Kumbh festival
Pantone's 2018 Colour of the Year is Ultra Violet
Unrest in West Bank and Gaza, schools closed and a general strike in response to America's recognition of Jerusalem as Israel's capital
Virtual currency Bitcoin rises in value, passing $18,440 on some exchanges
Brazilian spiritual healer João Teixeira de Faria accused of sexual abuse by four women, over 200 more come forward in next week
Former Italy national football coach Cesare Prandelli is appointed manager of Serie A club Genoa CFC replacing Ivan Juric; first time in history all 20 Serie A managers are all Italian
Mosconi Cup nine-ball pool, London, England: US ends Europe's 8-year run with an 11-9 win; MVP: Skyler Woodward (USA)
Australian states celebrate "Freedom Day" easing COVID-19 restrictions in Victoria and New South Wales
Breaking, the competitive form of breakdancing, confirmed as an Olympic sport for the Paris 2024 games
Coca-Cola named the world's No. 1 plastic polluter, in Break Free From Plastic's annual brand audit
Ghanaian President Nana Akufo-Addo wins a second term in the country's general elections
Most of California begins second lockdown as hospitals come under strain and the state records average of 21,000 COVID-19 cases a day
3,500 year-old Gilgamesh Dream Tablet goes on display in Iraq for the first time in 30 years after looted during Gulf War. Part of the Epic Gilgamesh, one of oldest known pieces of literature.
Chile becomes the 31st nation to legalize same-sex marriage
Mockumentary comedy "Abbott Elementary" premieres in the US, created and starring Quinta Brunson

After widespread public protests, China announces a major loosening of COVID-19 restrictions for the whole country, allowing home quarantine and scrapping QR codes, effectively ending China's zero-COVID policy
Democratic Senator Raphael Warnock wins re-election over Republican Herschel Walker in run-off for the Georgia Senate seat, taking the US Senate to a clearer 51-49 Democratic majority

Peruvian president Pedro Castillo ousted from office after trying to dissolve Congress, who were about to impeach him on corruption charges. Vice president, Dina Boluarte sworn in as country's first woman president.
Twenty-five right-wing extremists arrested in Germany suspected of organizing a coup to overthrow the government and install a monarchy, including prince Heinrich XIII and army officers
The China Jinping Underground Laboratory (CJPL), the world's deepest and largest underground laboratory at 2,400 meters begins operating below Jinping Mountains
Ghana's presidential election won by former president John Mahama of the National Democratic Congress, defeating Vice President Mahamudu Bawumia of the New Patriotic Party
Restored Notre Dame Cathedral re-opens after the 2019 fire with a VIP ceremony attended by world leaders in Paris, France
Roman orator and politicianMarcus Tullius Cicerois assassinated in Formiae by soldiers under the command ofMark Antony
Physician Sir Hans Soane becomes President of the Royal Society, succeeding SirIsaac Newton
NaturalistAlexander von Humboldtreports his discovery of the decrease in intensity of Earth's magnetic field from the poles to the equator in a memoir to the Paris Institute
James Madisonelected 4th US President, andGeorge Clintonre-elected Vice-President
Martin Van Burenelected the 8th President of the United States
Battle of Hartsville, Tenessee: Confederate ColonelJohn Hunt Morganand his cavalry capture garrison at Hartsville
Jesse Jamesfirst confirmed bank robbery - he and his brother rob a bank in Gallatin, Missouri, shots cashier in a revenge killing
W. S. GilbertandArthur Sullivan's comic opera "The Gondoliers" premieres at the Savoy Theatre, London
InventorLeo Baekelandpatents the first thermosetting plastic, Bakelite, sparking the birth of the plastics industry
David Lloyd Georgereplaces resigningH. H. Asquithas British Prime Minister at the head of a coalition Conservative government
US 42nd 'Rainbow' Infantry Division arrives in France with ColonelDouglas MacArthuramong its ranks
Noël Coward's play "Easy Virtue" premieres in NYC
Red Sox acquire the contract of 19-year-oldTed Williams
New York Yankees legendLou Gehrig, 36, is elected to Baseball Hall of Fame, only six months after his retirement
Adolf Hitlerissues "Nacht und Nebel Erlass" (Night and Fog Directive), against anti-Nazi resistance fighters in occupied territories; targets sent to concentration camps and/or murdered
WWII: German Siege of Tobruk, Libya ends after 8 months, asErwin Rommelwithdraws after losses and supply chain failures
Chinese Nationalist leaderChiang Kai-shekflees to Taiwan
Israel's PMBen-Gurionretires
Soviet artistic gymnastLarisa Latyninatakes her gold medal tally to 4 at the Melbourne Olympics; wins teams and individual all-round titles having earlier won vault and floor exercise sections
George Harrisonchanges his music publishing company's name from Mornyork to Harrisongs
Roman Catholic PopePaul VIand Orthodox Patriarch Athenagoras I simultaneously lift the mutual excommunications that led to the split of the two churches in 1054 during the Great Schism
Otis Reddingrecords his song "(Sittin' On) The Dock of the Bay", co-written and produced by guitarist Steve Cropper at Stax Studios in Memphis Tennessee
London Records releases Rolling Stones album "Beggar's Banquet" in US, the last duringBrian Jones' lifetime
Philippine's first ladyImelda Marcosstabbed and wounded by an assailant
Apple Records releasesPaul McCartney& Wings album "Band on the Run" in UK; the commercial and critical pinnacle of his post-Beatle work tops the charts in 7 countries
Romanian tennis starIlie Năstasewins his 4th season-ending ATP Masters Grand Prix title with a 6-2, 6-2, 6-1 rout ofBjörn Borgof Sweden in Stockholm

"Star Trek: The Motion Picture," the first movie of the series, premieres, directed by Robert Wise and starringWilliam ShatnerandLeonard Nimoy
51st Heisman Trophy Award:Bo Jackson, Auburn (RB)
PresidentJean-Claude Duvalierflees Haiti
Soviet leaderMikhail Gorbachevarrives in the US for a summit meeting withRonald Reagan
MLB Texas Rangers sign free-agent pitcherNolan Ryanto a one-year contract

Television adaptation of Tony Kushner's "Angels in America" directed byMike Nichols, starring Justin Kirk andAl Pacino, premieres on HBO
Diana DeGarmoreleases her debut album, "Blue Skies"
U.S. debut of the second part of two-part TV biopic miniseries "PopeJohn Paul II" on CBS
David Bowie’s final public appearance, at the opening of his musical "Lazarus" at New York Theatre Workshop, NYC

Annegret Kramp-Karrenbauer is elected the new leader of Germany's Christian Democratic Union party, replacingAngela Merkel

Court filings by US federal prosecutors and Special Counsel Robert Mueller againstMichael Cohenappear to implicate PresidentDonald Trumpon campaign fraud and Russian dealings
